summary: Record owner amendment A-9, remove the obsolete 2020-class viewer minimum, designate AMD Radeon 780M as the disclosed FR-2.5 benchmark baseline, and define fail-closed source-bound qualification without predeclaring a candidate verdict.

decisions.decision: Remove the 2020-class integrated-GPU minimum and use AMD Radeon 780M as the owner-designated hardware-disclosing benchmark baseline for the exact two-million-triangle, at-least-30-fps orbit profile. Treat the benchmark as acceptance evidence rather than a minimum supported-hardware claim, and require the qualifying label to match the unmasked renderer.
decisions.rationale: The owner determined that the former 2020-class target no longer represents the intended present-day baseline. Review found that earlier Radeon checkpoints were not bound to the exact candidate tree, profiler harness, timestamps, workload, and raw page/console observations. Those checkpoints are diagnostic only; this entry does not predeclare current-candidate qualification.

testEvidence.criterion: Current requirements and boundary documentation contain the A-9 baseline and no live 2020-class minimum
testEvidence.test: Require the canonical specification, question queue, README, CHANGELOG, and performance harness to agree on AMD Radeon 780M as an evidence baseline rather than minimum supported hardware.

red.outcome: RED: 1 test failed and 3 passed because the canonical specification still contained the 2020-class integrated-GPU requirement and the A-9 baseline records did not yet exist.

green.outcome: GREEN: 1 test file and all 5 tests passed, including the A-9 cross-document, qualifying-renderer, source-bound external-evidence, and no-stale-verdict contracts.

testEvidence.criterion: Owner-baseline publication is bound to the exact candidate source and profiler harness
testEvidence.test: Require the owner label to carry a pre-sample source identity, reject unstaged, untracked, changed, or tree-absent harness files, retain commit/tree/branch and independent harness hashes, validate canonical ordered timestamps, and prove a fully staged candidate's recorded tree becomes the committed HEAD tree.

red.outcome: EVIDENCE RED: adversarial review proved that the prior hardware profile lacked candidate source/tree identity, profiler-harness hashes, execution timestamps, expected workload, and retained page/console observations, so it could not qualify the current candidate.

green.outcome: GREEN: 3 test files and all 37 tests passed, including all 17 performance helper and wrapper cases. Clean and fully staged source identities are recorded; a fully staged candidate tree matches the tree after commit; missing, lexically-first unstaged, untracked, changed, ignored/tree-absent, or working-tree-divergent sources cannot publish; and the evidence envelope retains exact source, harness, workload, timestamps, immutable observations, and an immutable profile snapshot. This unit green proves the fail-closed publication contract, not owner-hardware acceptance.

testEvidence.criterion: Rejected or stale performance output cannot masquerade as qualifying evidence
testEvidence.test: Remove prior qualifying JSON before every preflight; recompute animation FPS, rendered FPS, and camera delta from raw observations; assess triangle count, degradation, renderer, trusted pointer moves, page errors, and console errors; and publish an explicit pass record only after all validation.

red.outcome: SOURCE RED: the earlier harness wrote viewer-performance-profile.json before its acceptance assertions, allowing a failing run or reused output directory to retain a qualifying-looking JSON file.

green.outcome: GREEN: 3 test files and all 37 tests passed, including all 17 performance helper and wrapper cases. Stale evidence is invalidated by the wrapper before Playwright configuration, discovery, web-server, or browser work and again before workload preflight; whitespace artifact-directory input resolves to the same default used by Playwright; and the wrapper forwards arguments without pnpm's separator. Non-finite, fractional, slow, short, wrong-count, rejected, and caller-spoofed evidence cannot publish; contradictory reported FPS and camera-delta values are rejected against raw observations; late mutations cannot alter the frozen accepted observation or profile snapshots; the owner label forces exactly 2,000,000 triangles; and output is written only after internal reassessment with explicit pass status and an empty acceptance-error list.

questions: Historical committed ledger entries remain immutable and accurately describe the earlier policy; A-9 and this append-only entry supersede only their hardware-qualification conclusion.

A-9 resolves the hardware policy only. The prior Radeon checkpoint is diagnostic; current-candidate FR-2.5 acceptance requires a retained external source-bound artifact. M2 also remains open until its provenance, review, package, and hosted similarity gates complete.
No owner similarity gate, comparison repository, workflow content, or prohibited source was opened or executed.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
